notes.text: Do not assume a specific `switch` value maps to `a` (e.g. that `false` selects `a`): that mapping is unrecorded for this gate.
An unconnected `switch` input is treated as a constant `false`.
While `enable` stays true, `out` alternates On/Off/On on a repeating cycle. `on_time`/`off_time` (default 0.5/0.5) are quantized to 0.1 s steps -- exactly 6 ticks at the 60 Hz tick rate.

The `expression` property is a small boolean-formula language: variable `x` (also `y`/`z`/`w` for this gate's other inputs), constants `true`/`false`, and operators `&` (AND), `|` (OR), `^` (XOR), `!` (NOT, usable as prefix or postfix).
notes.source: Reproduced from the Stormworks community wiki (Japanese).

notes.text: BOOL_FUNC_8 is the 8-input version of BOOL_FUNC_4: its `expression` variables are `x`,`y`,`z`,`w`,`a`,`b`,`c`,`d`. The expression syntax is otherwise identical to BOOL_FUNC_4.

If `out` is Off and `enable` is held On continuously for `charge_time` seconds, `out` turns On. If `out` is On and `enable` is held Off continuously for `discharge_time` seconds, `out` turns Off. It functions as an On/Off buffer with time hysteresis.
Despite the name, CAPACITOR has no analog charge/discharge curve. It is simply an On/Off input buffer with turn-on and turn-off delays.

The `channel` property is xmlDelta-encoded to the XML `@i` attribute and defaults to 1. storm-mcl omits `@i` entirely when `channel` is left at 1; round-tripping such a file through Stormworks itself (loading it and letting the game re-save it) reproduces the same omission, confirming Stormworks also treats an absent `@i` as channel 1.
notes.source: See the property-omission logic in src/core/exporters/xml-tree.ts.

notes.text: The `channel` property is xmlDelta-encoded to the XML `@i` attribute and defaults to 1. storm-mcl omits `@i` entirely when `channel` is left at 1; round-tripping such a file through Stormworks itself (loading it and letting the game re-save it) reproduces the same omission, confirming Stormworks also treats an absent `@i` as channel 1.
notes.source: See the property-omission logic in src/core/exporters/xml-tree.ts.

notes.text: Do not assume a specific `switch` value maps to `a` (e.g. that `false` selects `a`): that mapping is unrecorded for this gate.
An unconnected `switch` input is treated as a constant `false`.

COMPOSITE_TO_NUMBER treats its 32 composite input bits as bit 0 (LSB) through bit 31 (MSB) of a 32-bit pattern, then reinterprets that bit pattern as an IEEE-754 float. It is not a simple binary-to-integer conversion.

Composite channels not selected as a write target (i.e. outside `offset`..`offset+count-1`) are not overwritten with a new value; `out` passes the corresponding channel of `inc` through unchanged, matching COMPOSITE_WRITE_NUMBER's confirmed behavior.
Composite channels not selected as a write target (i.e. outside `offset`..`offset+count-1`) are not overwritten with a new value; `out` passes the corresponding channel of `inc` through unchanged.

COUNTER's three inputs are `up`, `down`, `reset` (booleans) -- there is no separate 'enable' input. Its properties are `increment` (default 1), `min`/`max` (default 0/100), and `reset_value` (default 0).
`out` changes by `increment` on every tick that `up` or `down` is held true -- continuously, not just on a rising edge -- clamped to [`min`, `max`].
DELTA outputs the difference between the current tick's `a` and the previous tick's `a` (this tick minus previous tick).

DIVIDE has a second output, `error` ("Divide by Zero") (boolean), in addition to `out` (number). Wire `error` explicitly if divide-by-zero must be detected -- `out`'s numeric value alone cannot signal that it happened.
When `b` is 0, `out` reads 0.
Division by zero inside `expression` yields 0 (not an error or Infinity). Modulo (`%`) by zero also yields 0.

`expression`'s formula language has one variable, `x` (bound to input `x`), constants `pi`/`pi2`, operators `+ - * / % ^`, and functions sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2/max/min/ceil/floor/round/abs/sgn/sqrt/len/len2/lerp/clamp. Trigonometric functions take/return radians. `sgn(0)` returns 1, not 0 (unlike the mathematical convention). `sqrt` of a negative number returns 0.
notes.source: Reproduced from the Stormworks community wiki (Japanese).

notes.text: FUNC_NUM_3 extends FUNC_NUM_1's `expression` variables to `x`,`y`,`z` (bound to inputs `x`,`y`,`z`). The formula syntax is otherwise identical to FUNC_NUM_1.
FUNC_NUM_3 also has three numeric properties, `p1`/`p2`/`p3`, usable inside `expression` as extra constants alongside `x`/`y`/`z`.
FUNC_NUM_8 extends FUNC_NUM_1's `expression` variables to `x`,`y`,`z`,`w`,`a`,`b`,`c`,`d` (bound to the 8 like-named inputs). The formula syntax is otherwise identical to FUNC_NUM_1.
At startup (load time), `q` is false.
When `j` and `k` are both true, `q` toggles every tick, matching the textbook JK-FF truth table. JK_FF has no separate clock input -- each tick's evaluation plays that role.
The `script` property is limited to ASCII characters and at most 8192 characters.
At startup (load time), `out` equals the `reset_value` property (default 0) -- the DSL makes this value explicit rather than leaving it implicit.
Reset takes priority over Set when `set` and `reset` are both true in the same tick.
NUM_JUNCTION routes `value` to exactly one of its two outputs, `on` or `off`, selected by `switch`. The non-selected output reads 0.
An unconnected `switch` input is treated as a constant `false`.
Do not assume a specific `switch` value maps to `a` (e.g. that `false` selects `a`): that mapping is unrecorded.
NUMBER_TO_COMPOSITE is the inverse of COMPOSITE_TO_NUMBER: it reinterprets `number`'s IEEE-754 float bit pattern as 32 composite boolean channels (bit 0 -> LSB through bit 31 -> MSB).
While `active` is false, the internal integral term is held at 0 and `out` is 0.
While `active` is false, the internal integral term is held at 0 and `out` is 0, matching PID.
The integral term accumulates `e * ki` (error times the `ki` input) each tick.
PULSE's output pulse width is 1 tick.
The `mode` property (default "rise") selects the trigger edge: "rise" (default), "fall", or "both".
At startup (load time), `q` is false.

SR_LATCH has two outputs, `q` and `not_q`. When `s` and `r` are both true, `q` is false and `not_q` is also false -- `not_q` does not equal `!q` in this case, breaking the usual complementary relationship.
Setting `min` equal to `max` makes THRESHOLD behave like an exact-equality check (EQUAL without an epsilon tolerance).
The `units` property (default "seconds"; the only other allowed value is "ticks") selects whether `duration` is interpreted in seconds or in ticks (60 Hz).
The `units` property (default "seconds"; the only other allowed value is "ticks") selects whether `duration` is interpreted in seconds or in ticks (60 Hz).
The `units` property (default "seconds"; the only other allowed value is "ticks") selects whether `duration` is interpreted in seconds or in ticks (60 Hz).
The `units` property (default "seconds"; the only other allowed value is "ticks") selects whether `duration` is interpreted in seconds or in ticks (60 Hz).
TOGGLE has no properties -- its trigger edge is not configurable, unlike PULSE (whose `mode` property selects rise/fall/both). Do not expect a `mode`-style property on TOGGLE.
Toggling is tied to the rising edge of `a`: each false-to-true transition flips `out`.
TOOLTIP_BOOLEAN has `on_label`/`off_label` properties (default "On"/"Off") in addition to `label`.

The tooltip text switches between `on_label` and `off_label` depending on the boolean `value` input -- this is the closest thing this gate has to conditional display; it is not a general expression-based condition.
TOOLTIP_NUMBER has an `error` ("Is Error") boolean input in addition to `value`.
When `error` is true, the tooltip renders an error/invalid state instead of the numeric value.
Do not assume a specific `switch` value maps to `a` (e.g. that `false` selects `a`): that mapping is unrecorded for this gate.
An unconnected `switch` input is treated as a constant `false`.
